accessibility_features.name: Wheelchair-accessible venue
accessibility_features.description: Entrances, seating areas, and key facilities are accessible to wheelchair users without requiring assistance.

accessibility_features.name: Accessible restroom
accessibility_features.description: Restroom with sufficient turning space, grab bars, and accessible fixtures that meet common accessibility standards.

accessibility_features.name: Step-free entrance
accessibility_features.description: Main entrance is level or ramped, with no steps or abrupt changes in level.

accessibility_features.name: Braille menu
accessibility_features.description: Food and drink menus available in braille for blind and low-vision guests.

accessibility_features.name: Within 500m of accessible public transit
accessibility_features.description: Located within a 500-meter route of at least one accessible public transit stop or station.

accessibility_features.name: Captions provided
accessibility_features.description: Real-time captions or accurate subtitles are provided for audio and video content.

accessibility_features.name: Sensory-friendly environment
accessibility_features.description: Lighting, sound levels, and visual stimuli are moderated, with options for quieter seating and reduced sensory overload.

accessibility_features.name: Plain-language materials
accessibility_features.description: Key written information is available in clear, plain language with supportive visuals where possible.

dietary_options.name: Vegan options available
dietary_options.description: The venue offers clearly labeled vegan main dishes and sides.

dietary_options.name: Vegetarian options available
dietary_options.description: A variety of vegetarian dishes are available beyond simple side salads.

dietary_options.name: Gluten-free options
dietary_options.description: Gluten-free dishes are available and prepared to reduce cross-contact where possible.

dietary_options.name: Dairy-free options
dietary_options.description: Dairy-free meals and desserts are available and clearly labeled.

dietary_options.name: Nut-free options
dietary_options.description: Nut-free dishes are available and prepared with careful cross-contact precautions.

dietary_options.name: Allergy-friendly
dietary_options.description: Kitchen and staff are prepared to safely accommodate a range of common food allergies.

dietary_options.name: Halal options
dietary_options.description: Halal-certified or compliant dishes are available and clearly labeled.

dietary_options.name: Kosher options
dietary_options.description: Kosher-certified or supervised dishes are available and clearly labeled.

donation_funds.name: Inclusive Cooking Classes
donation_funds.description: Supports free and low-cost inclusive cooking classes for community members with diverse access needs.

donation_funds.name: Community Dinners
donation_funds.description: Helps fund accessible community dinners featuring sliding-scale pricing and inclusive menus.

donation_funds.name: General Fund
donation_funds.description: Provides flexible support for the organization’s most urgent needs across all programs.

donation_funds.name: Accessibility Initiatives
donation_funds.description: Invests in training, assistive technology, and accessibility improvements across partner venues.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: One-Pan Lemon Herb Chickpeas (Gluten-Free & Vegan)
recipes.description: A bright, protein-packed one-pan meal with chickpeas, seasonal vegetables, and fresh herbs—perfect for a quick inclusive dinner.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chickpeas (or 1 can, drained and rinsed)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add chickpeas, bell peppers, and cherry tomatoes. Cook for 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season with lemon zest, lemon juice, oregano, basil, salt, and pepper.
Cook for another 3–4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Taste and adjust seasoning, then garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: Creamy Coconut Lentil Curry (Vegan)
recipes.description: A cozy, weeknight-friendly lentil curry with coconut milk and warming spices, ready in under 40 minutes.
ingredients: Cooked rice or quinoa, for serving
instructions: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat and sauté onion until translucent.
Add garlic and ginger; cook for 1 minute.
Stir in curry powder and cumin; toast for 30 seconds.
Add red lentils, coconut milk, vegetable broth, and diced tomatoes; stir well.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 18–20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender.
Season with salt and pepper, garnish with cilantro, and serve over rice or quinoa.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: Rustic Tomato Pasta Bake (Gluten-Free)
recipes.description: A comforting baked pasta made with gluten-free pasta, roasted tomatoes, and a simple dairy-light sauce.
ingredients: 1/2 cup shredded dairy-free or low-lactose cheese (optional)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions until just al dente; drain.
In a baking dish, combine cooked pasta, marinara sauce, cherry tomatoes, olive oil, oregano, basil, salt, and pepper.
Top with cheese if using.
Bake for 15–20 minutes until bubbly and lightly browned on top.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve warm.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: Sheet-Pan Rainbow Fajitas
recipes.description: Colorful sheet-pan fajitas with a mix of peppers, onions, and your choice of protein, perfect for serving a crowd of four.
ingredients: 1 lb sliced mushrooms or chicken strips
Optional toppings: salsa, avocado, dairy-free yogurt
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a sheet pan with parchment paper.
Spread bell peppers, onion, and mushrooms or chicken on the sheet pan.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat.
Bake for 15–18 minutes, stirring halfway, until vegetables are tender and protein is cooked through.
Warm tortillas and serve fajita mixture with desired toppings.

recipes.title: Slow-Simmered Inclusive Chili for a Crowd
recipes.description: A rich, layered chili with flexible toppings to accommodate vegan, gluten-free, and omnivore diners at the same table.
ingredients: 2 cans kidney beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot and sauté onion, garlic, bell pepper, and carrots until softened.
Stir in lentils, beans, crushed tomatoes, broth, chili powder, and smoked paprika.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer partially covered for 60–75 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper and serve with a toppings bar for different dietary needs.

recipes.title: 30-Minute Ginger Veggie Stir-Fry
recipes.description: A flexible stir-fry with a ginger-garlic sauce and customizable veggies, ideal for weeknight dinners.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat.
Add tofu and cook until lightly browned; remove and set aside.
Stir-fry vegetables until crisp-tender.
Return tofu to the pan and add soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and maple syrup.
Cook for 2–3 minutes more, then serve over rice.

recipes.title: Family-Style Herb Roast with Root Vegetables
recipes.description: A hearty roast with plenty of root vegetables, designed for mixed-diet households.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Toss vegetables with oil, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a baking sheet and roast 45–70 minutes, stirring once.
Add optional protein as desired and roast until cooked through.

recipes.title: 15-Minute Colorblock Salad with Crunchy Chickpeas
recipes.description: A visually intuitive salad with clearly separated components to support diners who prefer low-mixed textures.
ingredients: 1 cup canned chickpeas, rinsed and drained
1 carrot, grated or sliced into sticks
instructions: Toss chickpeas with olive oil and smoked paprika; pan-toast for 3–5 minutes until crisp.
Arrange greens, carrot, cucumber, and tomatoes in separate color blocks on a plate.
Top with crunchy chickpeas and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Gluten-Free Vegan Sheet-Pan Flatbread Pizza
recipes.description: A crowd-pleasing flatbread pizza using a gluten-free base and dairy-free cheese.
instructions: Preheat oven according to crust package instructions.
Spread tomato sauce over the crust.
Top with vegetables and dairy-free cheese; drizzle with olive oil.
Sprinkle with oregano and basil and bake until crust is crisp and toppings are cooked.

recipes.title: 45-Minute Stovetop Veggie Paella
recipes.description: A stovetop paella with saffron rice and seasonal vegetables, designed to be dairy-free and naturally gluten-free.
instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, and bell pepper in olive oil until softened.
Stir in rice, smoked paprika, and saffron; toast for 1–2 minutes.
Add broth, bring to a simmer, and cook uncovered for 15 minutes without stirring.
Add peas on top and cook 10 minutes more, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: No-Bake Chocolate Sunflower Bites
recipes.description: A nut-free, school-friendly treat sweetened with dates and cocoa powder.
instructions: Pulse dates and sunflower seeds in a food processor until crumbly.
Add cocoa powder, vanilla, and salt; process until mixture holds together.
Roll into bite-sized balls and chill before serving.

recipes.title: Accessible Overnight Oats with Texture Options
recipes.description: Customizable overnight oats with guidance on texture modifications and serving suggestions for different sensory needs.
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats (certified gluten-free if needed)
Toppings: fruit, seeds, nut or seed butter
instructions: Combine oats, plant-based milk, chia seeds, and maple syrup in a jar.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight.
Before serving, add toppings and adjust consistency with extra milk if needed.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: Stuffed Sweet Potatoes with Black Beans
recipes.description: Microwave-friendly stuffed sweet potatoes topped with smoky black beans and fresh salsa.
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Pierce sweet potatoes and microwave 8–10 minutes, turning halfway, until tender.
Warm black beans in a small pot with smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Split sweet potatoes open and fluff flesh with a fork.
Top with black beans, salsa, avocado, and cilantro, then serve.

recipes.title: Quick Dinner: Creamy Polenta Bowls with Roasted Vegetables
recipes.description: Comforting polenta bowls topped with simply roasted vegetables, adaptable for dairy-free diners.
ingredients: Optional: 1/4 cup grated cheese or dairy-free alternative
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss vegetables with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper, and roast 15–20 minutes.
Bring water or broth to a boil, whisk in polenta, and cook until creamy, about 5–8 minutes.
Stir in remaining olive oil and optional cheese; season to taste.
Serve polenta in bowls topped with roasted vegetables.

resource_articles.title: Creating Welcoming Dining Experiences for Guests with Visual Impairments
resource_articles.summary: Practical steps restaurants can take to support blind and low-vision guests from arrival to dessert.
resource_articles.content: This guide walks through each stage of a dining visit from the perspective of guests with visual impairments. It covers tactile wayfinding, staff orientation scripts, how to offer menu descriptions respectfully, and strategies for labeling buffet items. The article includes sample phrases staff can use and low-cost changes that dramatically improve independence and comfort.

resource_articles.title: Checklist: Low-Vision Friendly Menu Design
resource_articles.summary: A printable checklist to review font size, contrast, layout, and digital menu accessibility.
resource_articles.content: Use this checklist when updating printed and digital menus. It focuses on font size, line spacing, color contrast, plain language descriptions, and compatibility with screen readers. The checklist can be used by restaurant managers, designers, or volunteers supporting community meal programs.

resource_articles.title: Designing Sensory-Friendly Dining Spaces
resource_articles.summary: Guidance on lighting, sound, and layout to make dining rooms more comfortable for autistic and sensory-sensitive guests.
resource_articles.content: Sensory-friendly dining is about offering more choice and control, not about a single perfect environment. This article explains how to create quieter zones, adjust lighting, reduce visual clutter, and communicate expectations ahead of time. It includes a sample sensory-friendly statement you can adapt for your website or menu.

resource_articles.title: Sensory-Friendly Dining for Community Events
resource_articles.summary: Tips for community organizers hosting potlucks, fundraisers, and dinners with sensory-friendly options.
resource_articles.content: Community events can overwhelm guests with sensory processing differences. This guide offers practical ideas such as quiet corners, visual schedules, clear signage, and menu previews. It also suggests ways to train volunteers to offer support without drawing unwanted attention to guests.

resource_articles.title: Mobility Access in Small Kitchens and Cafés
resource_articles.summary: How to improve maneuverability, reduce tripping hazards, and support wheelchair users in tight spaces.
resource_articles.content: Even narrow kitchens can be made more accessible with thoughtful layout changes. This article discusses aisle width, flexible prep stations, and how to plan traffic flow so staff using mobility aids can navigate safely.

resource_articles.title: Captions, Interpreters, and Visual Cues for Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing Diners
resource_articles.summary: A primer on accessible communication in restaurants, from visual paging systems to captioning online menus.
resource_articles.content: This guide outlines options for communication access, including live captioning for virtual tastings, video remote interpreting, and simple visual paging systems. It also covers how to note these options on your website so guests can plan their visit.

resource_articles.title: Allergy-Friendly Kitchen Basics for Community Meals
resource_articles.summary: Step-by-step guidance on preventing cross-contact and communicating clearly with guests about allergens.
resource_articles.content: From labeling bulk ingredients to using separate utensils and color-coded cutting boards, this article provides concrete actions to reduce risk for guests with food allergies. It includes sample allergen statements and a simple training outline for volunteers.

resource_articles.title: Plain-Language Menus That Still Feel Special
resource_articles.summary: How to describe dishes clearly without losing the joy and story behind your food.
resource_articles.content: Plain-language menus help guests with cognitive disabilities, language learners, and anyone overwhelmed by jargon. This resource shares before-and-after examples of menu descriptions and offers tips for pairing icons and illustrations with text.

resource_articles.title: Planning an Accessible Community Dinner from Start to Finish
resource_articles.summary: A step-by-step toolkit for inclusive menu planning, space setup, and volunteer training.
resource_articles.content: This toolkit walks organizers through timelines, checklists, and sample forms for planning an inclusive community meal. Topics include budgeting for interpreters, choosing accessible venues, creating multiple seating options, and collecting feedback.

resource_articles.title: How Donations Expand Inclusive Cooking Classes
resource_articles.summary: Stories from participants whose confidence and skills grew through inclusive cooking programs.
resource_articles.content: Donor support helps us offer free and sliding-scale classes with interpreters, adaptive equipment, and transportation stipends. This article shares short stories from participants and outlines how recurring gifts sustain the program year-round.

resource_articles.title: Volunteer Orientation Script for Inclusive Kitchens
resource_articles.summary: A ready-to-use script to welcome new volunteers and introduce accessibility practices.
resource_articles.content: This script can be read or adapted by shift leads at the start of each volunteer session. It covers kitchen safety, allergy procedures, communication tips, and how to offer assistance respectfully to guests with disabilities.

resource_articles.title: Making Online Cooking Workshops Accessible for Low-Vision Participants
resource_articles.summary: Specific tips for camera angles, verbal description, and recipe formatting in virtual classes.
resource_articles.content: Online cooking classes can be highly accessible with a few thoughtful choices. This resource covers how to narrate actions clearly, adjust camera angles to show texture and color, and send recipes in screen-reader friendly formats ahead of time.

places.name: Central City Accessible Cafe

places.name: Accessible Riverside Eatery

places.name: Riverfront Diner Riverside
places.description: Classic diner facing the riverwalk with a mix of comfort food and a few plant-forward specials.

places.name: Riverside Vegan Kitchen

places.name: Downtown Kitchen Hub
places.description: A community kitchen in Downtown Springfield used for inclusive cooking classes, community dinners, and volunteer kitchen shifts.

places.name: East Market Community Food Hall

places.name: Springfield Inclusive Community Center

places.name: Central City Food Truck Park

places.name: Harborfront Seafood Grill
places.description: Seafood-focused restaurant on the Harborfront with a quiet indoor dining room and outdoor patio.

places.name: Other City Inclusive Bistro

volunteer_shift_signups.name: Maria Gonzalez
volunteer_shift_signups.additional_notes: Comfortable standing for most of the shift; prefers tasks with clear written instructions.

volunteer_shift_signups.name: Chris Patel

volunteer_shift_signups.name: Leah Kim
volunteer_shift_signups.additional_notes: Will arrive 10 minutes early; has food handler certification.

volunteer_shift_signups.name: Devon Smith

volunteer_shift_signups.name: Priya Rao

volunteer_shifts.title: Downtown Kitchen Hub – Saturday Morning Prep Shift (8:00–11:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Help prep ingredients, set up accessible workstations, and support inclusive cooking classes during the Saturday morning session.
required_skills: Comfort standing for up to 3 hours

volunteer_shifts.title: Downtown Kitchen Hub – Saturday Afternoon Community Dinner Prep (13:00–17:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Assist with batch cooking, labeling allergen-friendly dishes, and light cleanup before the evening community dinner.
required_skills: Openness to learning about inclusive kitchen practices

volunteer_shifts.title: Downtown Kitchen Hub – Sunday Brunch Kitchen Support (10:00–14:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Support the inclusive Sunday brunch program by preparing toppings, plating dishes, and helping maintain a clean, accessible kitchen.

volunteer_shifts.title: Downtown Kitchen Hub – Weeknight Class Kitchen Support (17:00–20:00, Wednesday)
volunteer_shifts.description: Weeknight shift setting up adaptive tools, portioning ingredients, and assisting instructors during an inclusive cooking class.

volunteer_shifts.title: Downtown Kitchen Hub – Thursday Morning Prep and Inventory (9:00–12:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Help with light food prep, checking inventory, and organizing labeled storage for allergy-friendly ingredients.
required_skills: Comfort with light lifting up to 10 kg
Ability to read basic labels in English

volunteer_shifts.title: Springfield Inclusive Community Center – Friday Evening Community Dinner Support (17:00–21:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Support an accessible community dinner with table setup, guest check-in, and guiding guests to seating and resources.
required_skills: Ability to stand and walk for most of the shift (seated tasks available on request)

volunteer_shifts.title: East Market Food Hall – Saturday Welcome Table & Wayfinding (10:00–13:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Greet guests at the community food hall, offer maps and large-print materials, and direct visitors to accessible seating.
required_skills: Comfort standing or sitting at a welcome table
Ability to read and explain a simple map layout

volunteer_shifts.title: Springfield Inclusive Community Center – Sunday Resource Fair Support (16:00–20:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Assist at an accessibility resource fair by staffing information tables, replenishing materials, and helping guests navigate the space.
required_skills: Comfort answering basic questions or finding staff who can
Ability to stay organized in a busy environment

volunteer_shifts.title: Accessible Riverside Eatery – Saturday Evening Front-of-House Support (17:00–21:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Support a busy Saturday dinner service by welcoming guests, offering large-print information, and guiding groups to accessible tables.
required_skills: Customer service experience helpful but not required
Comfort working in a lively environment
Ability to communicate clearly about accessibility options

volunteer_shifts.title: Riverside Vegan Kitchen – Sunday Brunch Line Support (11:00–14:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Join the kitchen team to prepare vegan brunch bowls, assemble allergen-aware plates, and keep the line running smoothly.
required_skills: Comfort working in a compact kitchen
Ability to read and follow ticket orders
Awareness of cross-contact risks for common allergens

volunteer_shifts.title: Central City Accessible Cafe – Weekday Pastry & Prep Support (9:00–11:00, Thursday)
volunteer_shifts.description: Help bake simple items, prep ingredients, and restock accessible service stations at the cafe.
required_skills: Ability to follow a simple prep list

volunteer_shifts.title: Central City Food Truck Park – Saturday Inclusive Dining Ambassadors (11:00–14:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Serve as an on-site ambassador, sharing information about the inclusive dining initiative and gathering feedback from guests.
required_skills: Comfort standing or strolling through the park
Ability to record short notes or survey responses

volunteer_shifts.title: Springfield Inclusive Community Center – Tuesday Admin & Data Entry Support (10:00–12:00)
volunteer_shifts.description: Assist staff with data entry, preparing plain-language handouts, and organizing volunteer files in an accessible format.
required_skills: Comfort working in a quiet office environment

events.title: Springfield Community Dinner: Flavors of Early Spring
events.description: A sliding-scale, family-style community dinner featuring early spring produce, clearly labeled vegan and gluten-free options, and table hosts trained in inclusive dining practices.

events.title: Downtown Neighbor Potluck & Recipe Swap
events.description: Bring a dish if you can, or simply bring yourself. This community dinner at Downtown Kitchen Hub includes a recipe swap focused on budget-friendly, allergy-aware meals.

events.title: Central City Community Dinner: Street Food Night
events.description: An outdoor-style community dinner at the Central City Food Truck Park featuring globally inspired small plates and accessible ordering support.

events.title: Winter Warmth Community Dinner at East Market
events.description: A cozy February community dinner with hot soups, stews, and quiet seating options inside the East Market Community Food Hall.

events.title: Springfield Midday Community Lunch
events.description: A relaxed, pay-what-you-can community lunch at Springfield Family Cafe with simple, comforting dishes.

events.title: Online Workshop: Intro to Inclusive Cooking at Home
events.description: Learn practical strategies for adapting recipes, tools, and kitchen setups so more people can comfortably participate in cooking at home.

events.title: Online Workshop: Allergy-Friendly Pantry Staples
events.description: Build an allergy-aware pantry with shelf-stable ingredients and substitutions for common allergens like gluten, dairy, nuts, and eggs.

events.title: Online Training: Sensory-Friendly Cooking for Families
events.description: Explore ways to adapt recipes, textures, and kitchen environments for family members with sensory processing differences.

events.title: In-Person Workshop: Safe & Accessible Knife Skills
events.description: Hands-on workshop at Downtown Kitchen Hub covering foundational knife skills, adaptive tools, and safety practices for mixed-ability kitchens.

events.title: In-Person Workshop: Allergy-Safe Baking Basics
events.description: Practice baking simple muffins and snacks without common allergens, using separate tools and clear labeling protocols.

events.title: Hybrid Training: Accessible Menu Design for Community Meals
events.description: A hybrid session for volunteers and organizers on planning menus that work for guests with visual, sensory, and dietary access needs.

events.title: Info Session: Partnering in the Inclusive Dining Guide
events.description: A free online info session for restaurant owners and managers interested in joining the Inclusive Dining Guide.

events.title: Inclusive Gastronomy Spring Fundraiser Gala
events.description: An accessible evening gala in Central City raising funds for inclusive cooking classes, with a multi-course tasting menu and stories from program participants.

events.title: Riverside Allergy-Friendly Dining Meetup
events.description: A casual meetup at Accessible Riverside Eatery for diners and caregivers to share tips and experiences about eating out with food allergies.
